For founders who've become the bottleneck

I build the operating systems that let you scale without breaking.

Somewhere between the whiteboard and the fifth Slack channel, everything started depending on you. I take the operational weight off your plate — the tracking, the timelines, the thousand small handoffs — so decisions stop waiting on you to remember them.

Sound familiar?

This is usually the moment someone reaches out.

You're still approving things at 11pm because you're the only one with the full picture.

Your calendar is full of meetings that only exist because nothing else is tracking status.

You hired for skill, not for structure — and now nothing talks to anything else.

The programme is funded, the clock is running, and you need someone who's actually run one before.

What I actually do

Two things, done properly, instead of ten things held together with hope.

Pillar one

Delivery on a date you can promise

  • A plan you can actually trust, with real owners on every piece
  • Someone watching for what's about to go wrong, before it does
  • Stakeholder updates that take 30 seconds to read, not a meeting to explain
  • A budget that holds, because someone's actually watching it
Pillar two

Systems that don't need you to run

  • So the next hire doesn't need a month of tribal knowledge to get moving
  • So nothing important lives only in your head or your DMs
  • Dashboards that tell you what's happening without you asking
  • A repeatable playbook, not a one-time fix
Proof, not promises

Five real problems. What they looked like, and what changed.

Africa Space Foundation × OASEAS — Co-Project Lead
Space Ungana 2025
100% on-time · 5% under budget
The problem

Three countries, one hard deadline, and no existing playbook to run a Pan-African summit on.

What changed

Built the plan from zero and ran it across a 6-person core team and 15 volunteers over four months. Kept 20+ risks in view so nothing became a surprise later. Went after sponsors directly instead of waiting for someone else to close the funding gap.

100+ people showed up, 95% said it delivered, and it still came in under budget with zero critical incidents.

Confidential client — Project Manager, Product
Fintech & Messaging Platform Launch
Launch-ready, fully aligned
The problem

Product, engineering, compliance, and marketing all building toward the same launch date — with no single source of truth for what "done" actually meant.

What changed

Owned the roadmap end to end, from the first scoping conversation through to launch-readiness review. Caught two scope changes before they quietly ate the timeline, and gave founders the impact analysis to decide fast.

Shipped through to product-ready stage with the whole team still aligned on what "shipping" meant.

Africa Space Foundation × OASEAS — Project Lead
Moon Base Café Hackathon
7 days, not 6 weeks
The problem

Seven days to pull off an event that usually takes four to six weeks — or don't run it at all.

What changed

Split a 4-person team by function, cut everything that wasn't essential, and ran speaker outreach and marketing in parallel instead of one after another.

100+ registrants from 5 African countries. It went out on time. Nothing broke.

DevMe Limited — Chief Operating Officer
DevMe Training Programs
95% closure · 25% less admin
The problem

Every cohort was being rebuilt from scratch, with no way to tell what was actually working.

What changed

Built the systems once so they could be reused every time — facilitator workflows, tracking, reporting. Put a CRM-based tracker in place that closed 95% of action items before they became someone's problem.

120+ people trained, a quarter less admin work, and three new partners who noticed the difference.

Univast Space — Operations Manager
Building the Operating Infrastructure From Zero
1,500+ students reached
The problem

Founders were spending more hours chasing updates than actually building the product.

What changed

Built the schedules, dashboards, and escalation paths from nothing, across three schools running programs at once.

1,500+ students reached, and the founders got their week back.

IVLead — Event Manager & Logistics Coordinator
Founders/CEOs Under 30 Leadership Summit
100% positive speaker feedback
The problem

150+ C-suite executives and young founders in one room, with 10+ high-profile speakers who each needed to feel personally taken care of.

What changed

Managed every speaker relationship end to end — travel, logistics, pre-event briefings — and built a run-of-show detailed enough that the day never felt like it was being figured out live.

150+ executives engaged all day, 10+ speakers coordinated with zero logistical issues, 100% positive speaker feedback.

About

A Mechatronics engineer who traded hardware for the systems founders run their companies on.

Three years across aerospace, EdTech, and early-stage startups — managing several part-time and contract engagements at once while delivering full project lifecycles in each. The engineering background stays with me in how I work: structured, risk-aware, built to hold up under load.

I've run programs from 100-person international summits to 150+ participant bootcamps, and I'm just as comfortable in a founder's Slack as I am in a stakeholder review with government and academic partners.

Based inAbuja, Nigeria (UTC+1)
LanguagesEnglish, Mandarin (HSK 3), French
EducationB.Eng. Mechatronics, First Class
CertificationsGoogle PM Certificate, Google AI Essentials
How we'd start

Four steps, usually one week.

01

Discovery call

We align on what's actually breaking, and what fixing it would look like.

02

Scoped proposal

I structure the scope, milestones, and delivery plan around your real constraints.

03

Signed agreement

Clear terms, clear deliverables, no ambiguity about what's in and out.

04

Start date confirmed

Usually within a week. I move at the pace your business needs.

Let's talk

Tell me what's breaking under the growth.

I'll tell you honestly whether I'm the right fix for it.
